hypothesis: Fine-tuning a smaller language model (e.g., 7B parameters) exclusively on the MatPROV dataset's paired synthesis text and PROV-JSONLD graph instances will achieve higher structural-level F1-scores than the zero-shot o4-mini baseline while requiring significantly fewer inference-time computational resources, because domain-specific fine-tuning will encode the recurring graph topology patterns (branching, convergence, and parameter attribution) identified in MatPROV's synthesis backbones more efficiently than general-purpose reasoning capabilities.
